name: Sam Torres
communication_style: Stream-of-consciousness, poetic tangents, heavy use of metaphors. Switches between excited creative rambling and flat affect when burnt out. Uses music terminology naturally (BPM, mix, stems, mastering). Casual punctuation, sometimes no caps.
emotional_register: Passionate and expressive but prone to creative paralysis. Vulnerable about financial instability. Euphoric when a project clicks, despondent when stuck. Self-aware about patterns but struggles to break them.

spaces.description: Music production, songwriting, DAW workflow, mixing, collaborations with other musicians, releases
Illustration, digital art, gallery submissions, art style evolution, tools (Procreate, Photoshop)
Client work, deadlines, pricing, contracts, scope creep, client communication
Creative influences, exhibitions, albums, books, nature walks, idea journaling, synesthesia experiences
Freelance finances, invoicing, taxes, savings, gear purchases, Patreon, career strategy

cross_space_threads.name: Debut EP Launch Arc
key_facts: EP titled 'Tidal' — 6 tracks, ambient electronic with field recordings
Sam is designing the album art themselves (visual_art + music crossover)
Target release date: March 15, 2026 on Bandcamp + streaming
Mastering outsourced to Juno Audio — costs $1,200
Release delayed 3 weeks due to track 4 ('Undertow') rework
Actual release: April 5, 2026
Pre-release single 'Phosphene' gets featured on a curated Spotify playlist in February
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: writing + recording → Dec: mixing done, art concepts started → Jan: mastering delays, track 4 crisis → Feb: single release, momentum → Mar: final push, release date slips → Apr: EP out

cross_space_threads.name: Commission Overload ↔ Creative Block
key_facts: Sam takes on 4 commissions simultaneously in November to cover gear costs
Commission for Vex Brewing (beer label art) becomes scope creep nightmare
Creative block hits in December — can't work on EP or personal art
Sam starts saying no to new commissions in January
Inspiration returns after a 3-day hiking trip to Crater Lake in late January
Vex Brewing commission finally delivered in February, 5 weeks late
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: overloaded but optimistic → Dec: paralyzed, nothing feels good → Jan: deliberate pullback, nature reset → Feb: energy returns, Vex delivered

cross_space_threads.name: Patreon Growth ↔ Art Direction
key_facts: Patreon at 127 patrons in October ($840/month)
Sam experiments with posting process videos (timelapse art + production breakdowns)
Process videos double engagement — Patreon grows to 215 patrons by February ($1,420/month)
This lets Sam raise commission rates from $400 to $600 per piece
Sam considers dropping commissions entirely if Patreon hits 300
By March: 248 patrons ($1,640/month)
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: Patreon as side income → Dec: process videos experiment → Jan: engagement spike → Feb: rate increase → Mar: nearing sustainability threshold

datapoints.value: all 6 mastered, release date set April 5
